articles.title: Late Night Mines Trump’s Million Mentions in the Epstein Files
articles.abstract: “A million times? There’s not even that many references to Hamlet in the play ‘Hamlet,’” Jordan Klepper said on “The Daily Show.”

multimedia.caption: Jordan Klepper joked that it might be easier to call the Epstein files “the Trump files featuring Jeffrey Epstein.”

articles.title: Una hazaña olímpica: ver a una superestrella de k-pop en Milán
articles.abstract: En los Juegos Olímpicos de Invierno abundan los famosos. Para los fans de Sunghoon, del grupo coreano Enhypen, llegar a ver a su ídolo en Milán es toda una prueba de resistencia.

multimedia.caption: Desde la semana pasada, un flujo constante de fans se ha reunido en el exterior de un hotel de Milán para ver a Sunghoon, una estrella de k-pop que está de visita con motivo de los Juegos Olímpicos.
multimedia.copyright: Alessandro Grassani para The New York Times

articles.title: Top Border Official Praised Agent Who Shot Chicago Woman, Evidence Shows
articles.abstract: A Border Patrol agent shot Marimar Martinez five times, claiming that she tried to run him over. Newly released videos and text messages reveal fresh details about what happened.

articles.title: Bangladesh Holds Elections on Thursday. Here’s What to Know.

articles.title: Rusia podría tomar ciudades clave de Ucrania
articles.abstract: Las tropas rusas han avanzado a un ritmo glacial en los últimos meses, pero los avances en el sur y el este de Ucrania podrían dar a Moscú una ventaja en las conversaciones de paz mediadas por EE. UU.
org_facet: Center for Strategic and International Studies

articles.title: Man Accused of Killing His Father Said He Was on a ‘Mission From God’
articles.abstract: In a courtroom in Ireland, prosecutors detailed the rambling, manic confessions of Henry McGowan, the New York man charged with murdering his father in a luxury hotel.

articles.title: Trump Wants to Revive Shipping. Investors Are Slow to Back Him.
articles.abstract: A French logistics behemoth promised $20 billion for the United States, but a year into President Trump’s second term, only a fraction of the money has arrived.

multimedia.caption: “He’s going to be investing $20 billion into the United States because of the election,” President Trump said last year when Rodolphe Saadé, the chief executive of the shipping company CMA CGM, visited the White House.
multimedia.copyright: Maansi Srivastava for The New York Times

articles.title: California Man Sentenced to 4 Years for Covert Work on China’s Behalf
articles.abstract: The man, Mike Sun, corresponded with Chinese government officials, monitored the visit of Taiwan’s president to California and backed the election of a city council member, according to court documents.

multimedia.caption: Tsai Ing-wen, who was then the president of Taiwan, came to California in 2023. During her visit, she met with Kevin McCarthy, a California Republican who was the speaker of the House at that time.

articles.title: Vance Deletes Post Recognizing Armenian Genocide
articles.abstract: President Trump has not recognized the Armenian genocide. The vice president’s office called the post an error.

articles.title: Highlights From Ilia Malinin’s Short Program
articles.abstract: With the nervousness of a first-time Olympian behind him, figure skater Ilia Malinin delivered his best routine of the Milan-Cortina Games on Tuesday, bouncing back from two relatively shaky performances in the team event to finish first in the short program with an unforgettable flourish.
articles.byline: By Alice Fang, Jon Huang, Eden Weingart, Dan Powell and Elisheba Ittoop
